name: Coda
one_liner: Coda is an all-in-one doc/workspace platform (docs that combine text, tables, and apps); its REST API lets you read and write docs, tables, rows, pages, formulas, controls, and trigger automations.
access_notes: Any Coda user (free or paid workspace) can generate an API token from Account Settings instantly, no payment or approval. OAuth2 is available for Pack/app integrations. API access respects the token owner's workspace role.
api_notes: RESTful API at base https://coda.io/apis/v1. ~50+ endpoints across Folders, Docs, Doc structure/Pages, Tables & Views, Rows (insert/upsert/update/delete), Columns, Formulas, Controls, Account, and Analytics. Mutations are asynchronous (HTTP 202 with request IDs). Tiered rate limits (reads ~100 req/6s, writes ~10 req/6s).
mcp_notes: No vendor-shipped MCP server found. Multiple community MCP servers exist (e.g. orellazri/coda-mcp, TJC-LP/coda-mcp-server, universal-mcp/coda), plus Pipedream/Merge hosted connectors. All explicitly unaffiliated with Coda.
main_blocker: None significant. Self-serve token is instant and free. Minor considerations: asynchronous write model (202 + request IDs) and moderate rate limits. Note Coda was acquired and docs now redirect to docs.superhuman.com, though the API host remains coda.io/apis/v1.

evidence.claim: REST API at https://coda.io/apis/v1 with resources for docs, tables, rows, pages, columns, formulas, controls, and automation triggering; async mutations via HTTP 202; tiered rate limits.
API is available to all users free of charge; tokens are generated from Account Settings and can be scoped to all docs or a specific doc/table.
OAuth2-based authentication is documented for building Packs/integrations with other services.

open_questions: Exact webhook surface: Coda supports webhook-triggered automations and the API can trigger automations, but there is no first-class outbound webhook subscription API. Post-acquisition (Superhuman/Grammarly) long-term stability of the coda.io API host is uncertain.
